danger_signals.visual_form: trees, flags, snowmen, sledders, other skiers rendered on downhill slope
danger_signals.meaning: collision hazards that cause tumble/fall and time loss
collision object that launches player airborne and causes speed reduction

danger_signals.visual_form: flag positioning relative to player trajectory
danger_signals.meaning: passing on incorrect side triggers snowplow animation and forced deceleration
must reach finish line before expiration or fail run

danger_signals.visual_form: player character tumbling/falling during airborne freestyle attempt
danger_signals.meaning: botched aerial maneuver results in crash and time loss

safety_signals.meaning: safe to accelerate and maintain speed

safety_signals.visual_form: player passing flag on correct side
safety_signals.meaning: maintains momentum and speed; prevents snowplow penalty

safety_signals.visual_form: smooth downhill scrolling and sprite animation flow
safety_signals.meaning: player is on optimal path and avoiding speed-reducing collisions
bonus points earned without tumble penalty

safety_signals.visual_form: timer display showing generous seconds remaining
safety_signals.meaning: adequate cushion to reach finish line safely

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: competing skiers and sledders on slope act as moving obstacles but not faction antagonists
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: player character sprite with ski equipment; animated skiing stance

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Snowy Hill, Steep Peak, Mount Nasty named designations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: three distinct mountain environments with progressively hazardous layouts and obstacle density

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Snowy Hill environment with sparse obstacle distribution and gentle slope angle
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: slower animation speed; fewer competing skiers onscreen; wider safe passages between flags

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Steep Peak environment with moderate obstacle density and steeper visual perspective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased animation speed; more competing skiers; tighter flag spacing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mount Nasty environment with dense obstacles and extreme slope perspective
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: rapid animation; maximum competitor skiers; minimal safe passages; aggressive mogul placement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: transition to solo run segments with cleared competitor sprites
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: solo runs feature only player character against clock; points convert to bonus timer seconds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: player sprite animation shifts to tumble/fall frames
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: immediate speed loss; temporary animation lock; time penalty applied; return to upright skiing posture

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scrolling speed of background and slope increases; sprite animation frame rate accelerates
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player sprite leans forward in skiing posture; smoother downslope progression

environmental_storytelling.example: Three named mountains (Snowy Hill, Steep Peak, Mount Nasty) communicate skill progression through environmental naming and visual density escalation
Trees, snowmen, sledders, and competing skiers populate slopes naturally; recreate real ski resort hazard environment without explicit text
Scrolling perspective angle, animation frame rate, and sprite scaling communicate descent speed and mountain steepness; faster scrolling = steeper terrain = higher danger
Mogul placement density increases across mountain difficulty levels; visually signals terrain roughness and skill requirement without text
Flag spacing and angling on slope create visual 'puzzle' path; player must visually parse correct trajectory before committing to movement

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: upright skiing posture; steady animation frame cadence; forward lean in sprite art
tumble/fall animation; temporary loss of control; animation lock until recovery
sprite launches vertically on mogul impact; performs trick animation during flight; vulnerable to botched landing
snowplow animation trigger; visible deceleration in scrolling speed; forced slowdown penalty
downslope movement; collision hazard role; animation matches player skiing style
stationary or slow-moving hazard; requires evasion or jump clearance

notes: Slalom is a single-player sports racing game with no faction conflict or political dimension. World-state communication relies entirely on environmental design (mountain difficulty levels), animation speed modulation, and perspective shifts to convey speed, danger, and terrain hazard. The game communicates through kinetic/visual means rather than narrative or symbolic systems. Danger and safety signals are entirely performance-based: collisions, timing pressure, and obstacle avoidance mechanics. No explicit color-coded faction systems or political alignment markers exist. The three mountains serve as progression/difficulty stages rather than rival factions.
